notes: Static cross-cloud risk rules consumed by v0.6+ FinOps decision tools. Each entry has an id (stable for cross-references), a cloud filter ('any' or a specific cloud), a trigger_id (mapped to a Python function in caveats.py), a human-readable message, and a severity ('info', 'warn', 'block'). Block-severity caveats prevent a target from being recommended; warn caveats flag a real risk; info caveats are advisory only.

caveats.message: Target cloud equals source cloud — no migration to assess.
OCI A1.Flex (Always Free + cheapest) is ARM (Ampere) — verify your x86 AMIs / images can be rebuilt for ARM before relying on these prices.
3-year RI / Savings Plan / CUD commitments are not portable across clouds. Pilot the target cloud at on-demand for 1-3 months before committing.
Internet egress >100 TB/month typically benefits from Direct Connect / ExpressRoute / Interconnect / FastConnect — not modeled here. Real cost may be 30-50% lower than these estimates.
Multi-AZ flag doubles compute totals on every cloud (sync replicas across two zones). Storage stays at 1× because object/block storage is usually cross-AZ at base price.
No compute items in inventory — assessment is storage / object / database / egress only. Compute cost is the largest line item in most workloads; consider whether you've inventoried it.
